title: tabernaemontana luciliae
intro: Tabernaemontana luciliae, also known as the Philippine violet wood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids, which are known for their medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against cell damage. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
